Meteoalarm.Warnings.Kafka.description: Kafka producer endpoint for Meteoalarm CloudEvents on the `meteoalarm` topic keyed by `{identifier}`.

Meteoalarm.Warnings.Mqtt.description: MQTT 5 producer endpoint for Meteoalarm CloudEvents using the alert topic hierarchy and retain settings declared in this manifest.

Meteoalarm.Warnings.Amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 producer endpoint for Meteoalarm warning CloudEvents on the configured broker address.

Meteoalarm.WeatherWarning.name: WeatherWarning
Meteoalarm.WeatherWarning.description: A severe weather warning from the EUMETNET Meteoalarm system, aggregating warnings from 30+ European national meteorological services. Each warning follows the CAP (Common Alerting Protocol) structure with awareness levels and hazard types.

Meteoalarm.Warnings.description: Events from Meteoalarm covering European warning areas. Alert and incident records describe the current upstream hazard state and update when the provider changes that state.

Meteoalarm.Warnings.mqtt.description: MQTT 5 variant of the Meteoalarm events. It carries the same alert semantics as the base events with MQTT topic routing and retain behavior declared per message.

Meteoalarm.Warnings.amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 variant of the Meteoalarm weather-warning events for queue-oriented consumers and Azure Service Bus deployments.

subject.description: AMQP message subject — stable entity identifier matching the CloudEvent subject and Kafka key for cross-transport correlation.

country.description: Country feed slug for Service Bus subscription filters and AMQP consumer routing.

severity.description: CAP severity level for Service Bus subscription filters and AMQP consumer routing.

awareness_type.description: Meteoalarm awareness type slug for Service Bus subscription filters and AMQP consumer routing.

Meteoalarm.WeatherWarning.name: WeatherWarning
Meteoalarm.WeatherWarning.description: A severe weather warning from the EUMETNET Meteoalarm system. Contains CAP-structured alert data with awareness level and type, geographic area with EMMA/NUTS geocodes, and multilingual info blocks.

schema.name: WeatherWarning
schema.description: A severe weather warning from the EUMETNET Meteoalarm system, aggregating warnings from 30+ European national meteorological services. Each warning follows the CAP (Common Alerting Protocol) structure with awareness levels and hazard types.

identifier.description: The unique CAP alert identifier assigned by the issuing national meteorological service.

sender.description: The identifier of the issuing national meteorological service (e.g., 'opendata@dwd.de').

sent.description: The date and time when the warning was issued, in ISO-8601 format.

status.description: The CAP alert status. 'Actual' for real warnings, 'Test' for test messages.

descriptions.Actual: Provider value `Actual` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Exercise: Provider value `Exercise` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.System: Provider value `System` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Test: Provider value `Test` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Draft: Provider value `Draft` for this coded alert field.

msg_type.description: The CAP message type indicating the nature of the alert.

descriptions.Alert: Provider value `Alert` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Update: Provider value `Update` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Cancel: Provider value `Cancel` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Ack: Provider value `Ack` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Error: Provider value `Error` for this coded alert field.

scope.description: The CAP scope of the alert. Typically 'Public' for weather warnings.

descriptions.Public: Provider value `Public` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Restricted: Provider value `Restricted` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Private: Provider value `Private` for this coded alert field.

country.description: Country feed slug where the warning applies (for example germany or france). Matches the {country} MQTT topic axis.

event.description: The weather event description, typically in the national language of the issuing service (e.g., 'STURMBÖEN', 'Thunderstorm').

category.description: The CAP alert category. 'Met' for meteorological warnings.

descriptions.Met: Provider value `Met` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Geo: Provider value `Geo` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Safety: Provider value `Safety` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Security: Provider value `Security` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Rescue: Provider value `Rescue` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Fire: Provider value `Fire` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Health: Provider value `Health` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Env: Provider value `Env` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Transport: Provider value `Transport` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Infra: Provider value `Infra` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.CBRNE: Provider value `CBRNE` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Other: Provider value `Other` for this coded alert field.

severity.description: Native CAP severity level (Minor, Moderate, Severe, Extreme, or Unknown). Matches the {severity} MQTT topic axis without further bucketing.

descriptions.Extreme: Provider value `Extreme` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Severe: Provider value `Severe` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Moderate: Provider value `Moderate` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Minor: Provider value `Minor` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Unknown: Provider value `Unknown` for this coded alert field.

urgency.description: The CAP urgency level indicating the time-frame for protective action.

descriptions.Immediate: Provider value `Immediate` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Expected: Provider value `Expected` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Future: Provider value `Future` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Past: Provider value `Past` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Unknown: Provider value `Unknown` for this coded alert field.

certainty.description: The CAP certainty level indicating the confidence in the forecast.

descriptions.Observed: Provider value `Observed` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Likely: Provider value `Likely` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Possible: Provider value `Possible` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Unlikely: Provider value `Unlikely` for this coded alert field.
descriptions.Unknown: Provider value `Unknown` for this coded alert field.

headline.description: A brief human-readable headline summarizing the warning, often in the national language.

description.description: A detailed description of the weather warning, often in the national language.

instruction.description: Recommended protective actions for the public.

effective.description: The date and time when the warning becomes effective, in ISO-8601 format.

onset.description: The expected date and time of onset of the weather event, in ISO-8601 format.

expires.description: The date and time when the warning expires, in ISO-8601 format.

web.description: A URL to the full warning details on the issuing service's website.

contact.description: Contact information for the issuing meteorological service.

awareness_level.description: The Meteoalarm awareness level as a string combining the numeric level and color, e.g., '2; yellow; Moderate'. Values range from '1; green' (no significant weather) to '4; red' (very dangerous).

awareness_type.description: Meteoalarm awareness type label normalized to lowercase kebab-case for MQTT topic routing (for example wind, snow-ice, thunderstorm, flooding). Matches the {awareness_type} MQTT topic axis.

area_desc.description: A textual description of the affected geographic area.

geocodes.description: A semicolon-separated list of geocode values (EMMA_ID or WARNCELLID) identifying the specific warning zones.

language.description: The language of the info block used to populate this event (e.g., 'de-DE', 'en-GB').

awareness_type_raw.description: Raw Meteoalarm awareness_type parameter value as provided by CAP, for example "2; Snow/Ice".

description: Meteoalarm publishes official weather warnings from the European Meteoalarm network for European warning areas. These events help consumers monitor hazards, route notifications, and correlate public-warning updates without polling the upstream source directly.

documentation.description: Source documentation for Meteoalarm, including upstream feed context and bridge deployment notes.
